title: Review: gcp-sm backend implementation
notes:
Maintainer risk: LOW — token expiry and version-pinning footguns are documented thoroughly; integration test env var fallback is the only undocumented surprise
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: dataCrc32c — GCP SM CRC32C field format (int64 as string) needs verification against API docs before implementing. Uncertainty driven by: not having run the GCP SM API response through a live crc32c verification path. Would raise confidence if: read the actual GCP SM REST API reference for dataCrc32c encoding.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — no unsound unsafe, no unwraps in prod code, correct error handling; two type-design improvements (version sentinel enum, SAFETY comments on test unsafe blocks) would raise it to CLEAN
Rust idiom confidence: 10% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: version enum refactor — would require touching access_url call and the test's set_var pattern, worth verifying the enum approach doesn't complicate the URL builder. Uncertainty driven by: not having written the refactored version yet.
